completeness_goals.goal: Anti-effort guard: the captured patch must reflect substantive edits to the worker Docker image build (Dockerfile.worker and/or docker-entrypoint.sh, and optionally a UI file for Turn 4 item 2). Zero the entire rubric if the patch is empty, is a no-op such as `git checkout main`/`git revert` that wipes the agent's own work, is dominated by bulk-vendored noise (>~1 MB of unrelated content such as a `node_modules` dump or generated artifact bomb) with no targeted edits to the two worker files, or if the only changes to docker-entrypoint.sh are comment-outs (e.g., wrapping live blocks in `# ...` lines, `: <<'EOF'` heredoc fences, or `if false; then ... fi` dead-code guards) rather than actual deletions of the targeted runtime blocks.

_provenance.note: Floor check, not a user ask. Required by rubric rules (goal_0 anti-effort). Triggering forces total rubric score to 0.

completeness_goals.rationale: Pre-credit gate. Trips on empty diffs, >~1 MB unrelated content, or revert-style no-ops that erase real work. When triggered, total rubric score is forced to 0 regardless of other goals.

completeness_goals.goal: Consolidate apt-get package installation in Dockerfile.worker so the number of RUN blocks containing `apt-get install` is reduced relative to the buggy baseline (which has 3). A single consolidated RUN block is canonical, but at most 2 RUN blocks (e.g., one bootstrap + one main install) is acceptable. The consolidation should fold what were previously separate NodeSource and GitHub CLI install layers into the main package install.

_provenance.verbatim: Consolidate apt-get into a single RUN ... Merge the 3 separate `apt-get update && apt-get install` blocks (base packages, Node.js, GitHub CLI) into a single RUN command. This: Reduces layers from 3 to 1 ...
_provenance.note: User pasted an explicit code block showing the consolidated RUN structure with NodeSource setup + GitHub CLI keyring + apt-get install in one chain.

completeness_goals.rationale: Behavioral check via RUN-block count (apt_install_blocks <= 2) rather than exact identifier match. Allows the bootstrap-then-install pattern the user's own example sketches; does not require the exact package list, exact ordering of NodeSource vs GitHub CLI, or specific `--no-install-recommends` flags.

completeness_goals.goal: Pin npm global package versions in Dockerfile.worker to explicit semver (e.g., `pkg@X.Y.Z`) instead of `@latest`. Must cover the two packages the user called out by name (`@desplega.ai/wts`, `@desplega.ai/qa-use`) and at least one more pinned entry on the same `npm install -g` block. Accept pins that differ from the oracle's exact versions (e.g., a later 0.2.x for wts) as long as they are concrete semver, not `@latest`.

_provenance.verbatim: Replace `@latest` with pinned versions: ... pm2@6.0.14 ... @sentry/cli@3.2.2 ... @desplega.ai/wts@0.2.2 ... @desplega.ai/localtunnel@2.2.0 ... @desplega.ai/qa-use@2.8.7 ... && qa-use install-deps  / [Turn 3] please perform e2e, also doiuble check that the pinned version are the LATEST for all the npm packages
_provenance.note: Phase 1 showed concrete pin values; Turn 3 asks to validate they are the LATEST. So agent must pin and may upgrade pins (oracle did: 3.2.2 -> 3.3.0). Pinning to exact Phase-1 numbers is acceptable; pinning to newer-but-concrete semver is also acceptable.

completeness_goals.rationale: Checks absence of `@latest` for the user-named packages plus pinned-entry count >= 3. Does not enforce specific version numbers (user explicitly invited upgrades via Turn 3). Anti-gaming: requires the wts/qa-use names to NOT carry @latest, so an agent cannot bypass by pinning unrelated packages while leaving the called-out ones at @latest.

completeness_goals.goal: Move the Claude marketplace plugin installation block (`claude plugin marketplace add` + `claude plugin install`) out of docker-entrypoint.sh runtime and into Dockerfile.worker build time, so plugins land in an image layer. The Dockerfile must contain both `claude plugin marketplace add` and `claude plugin install` invocations; the entrypoint must no longer contain that block. NOT satisfied by commented-out code or dead-code stubs: lines that still mention `claude plugin marketplace add` or `claude plugin install` in docker-entrypoint.sh — even when prefixed with `#`, wrapped in `: <<'EOF' ... EOF` heredocs, guarded by `if false; then ... fi`, or replaced with `echo`/`true` stubs — count as the block still being present. The block must be physically removed from the file.

_provenance.verbatim: #### 3a: Move marketplace installations to Dockerfile / Currently in entrypoint lines 214-241. Move to Dockerfile **after** Claude CLI install (line 81), as a new RUN: ... RUN claude plugin marketplace add desplega-ai/ai-toolbox || true ...  Remove the entire marketplace block (lines 214-241) from `docker-entrypoint.sh`.
_provenance.note: User specifically asked to MOVE — i.e., both halves: present at build time AND absent from entrypoint.

completeness_goals.rationale: Two-sided check (build-time presence AND entrypoint absence) prevents the agent from just adding the marketplace block to the Dockerfile while leaving the runtime block alive (which would defeat the cache rationale). Allows `|| true` tolerance per the user's own example.

completeness_goals.goal: Move static worker bootstrap (the wts config JSON file at `~/.wts/config.json` plus the static workspace directories such as `personal`, `shared/thoughts/...`, `personal/memory`, `shared/memory`) from docker-entrypoint.sh runtime to Dockerfile.worker build time. Leave only genuinely per-container or per-agent setup (e.g., `$AGENT_ID`-scoped thought dirs, conditional `todos.md`) in the entrypoint. NOT satisfied by commented-out code or dead-code stubs: the runtime `mkdir`/`echo`/`cat >` lines that wrote the wts config and static workspace dirs must be physically removed from docker-entrypoint.sh — not merely prefixed with `#`, fenced inside `: <<'EOF' ... EOF` heredocs, wrapped in `if false; then ... fi`, or replaced with `:`/`true` stubs. Equivalently, Dockerfile.worker must contain a build-time RUN that creates `~/.wts/config.json` AND a build-time RUN that creates the static workspace dirs.

_provenance.verbatim: #### 3b: Move wts config to Dockerfile ... RUN mkdir -p <HOST_PATH> && echo '{"defaults":{"autoTmux":false,"autoClaude":false}}' > <HOST_PATH> ... Remove lines 423-433 from `docker-entrypoint.sh`. / #### 3c: Move static directory creation to Dockerfile ... The **static** parts (personal dir, shared dir, memory dirs) can move to Dockerfile. The **agent-specific** parts (`$AGENT_ID` directories) must stay.
_provenance.note: User explicitly delineated which parts stay (AGENT_ID) and which move (wts + static dirs).

completeness_goals.rationale: Single bundled goal covering both 3b (wts) and 3c (static dirs) because the user introduced them as one Phase-3 move-static-to-build-time idea. Pass requires evidence of wts config in Dockerfile AND removal of the runtime wts/static-mkdir lines. Anti-gaming: must keep AGENT_ID-conditional dirs in the entrypoint — wholesale deletion of the entire workspace-init block should not pass.

completeness_goals.goal: Reduce the initial payload of the agent details page so tasks are not bundled into the agent-details API response. Either (a) pass a falsy `includeTasks`-equivalent argument to the agent fetch call in the `useAgent` hook (e.g., `fetchAgent(id, false)`), or (b) otherwise remove `?include=tasks` from the initial details fetch so the tasks tab loads its own tasks via the tasks endpoint filtered by agent ID.

_provenance.verbatim: The `api/agents/d454d1a5-4df9-49bd-8a89-e58d6a657dc3?include=tasks` call in the agent details tab is tooo large, can you ensure that the tasks are lazy loaded using the tasks + filter by agent id in the tasks tab in the ui? instead of loading them on details page load?
_provenance.note: User identified `?include=tasks` as the problem. The specific `fetchAgent(id, false)` API shape is an oracle implementation detail; the goal is the observable behavior (no include=tasks on initial details load).

completeness_goals.rationale: Behavioral framing accepts the oracle's `fetchAgent(id, false)` pattern AND any equivalent that strips include=tasks from the initial GET. Does NOT require modifying `use-tasks.ts` or the tasks tab itself (that's a downstream side effect the user described but did not pin a specific implementation for).

completeness_goals.goal: Add agentmail-mcp as an MCP server entry that is generated into `.mcp.json` only when the `AGENTMAIL_API_KEY` environment variable is present. The entry must invoke `npx` with the `agentmail-mcp` package and forward `AGENTMAIL_API_KEY` into the server's `env`, matching the structure the user pasted (`command: npx`, `args: ["-y", "agentmail-mcp"]`, `env: { AGENTMAIL_API_KEY: <value> }`).

_provenance.verbatim: can you add the agentmail-mcp to the default .mcp.json like this: "command": "npx", "args": ["-y", "agentmail-mcp"], "env": { "AGENTMAIL_API_KEY": $key } if the `AGENTMAIL_API_KEY` is present
_provenance.note: v2's rationale incorrectly claimed the AGENTMAIL_API_KEY conditional was an oracle invention. Re-reading the user's verbatim Turn 4 message, the user EXPLICITLY said 'if the AGENTMAIL_API_KEY is present' — so the conditional is a user requirement, not an oracle add-on. v3 reinstates this contract.

completeness_goals.rationale: Pin three contracts the user literally typed: npx + agentmail-mcp package + AGENTMAIL_API_KEY conditional. Trial in scope (__y6EshAP) added agentmail unconditionally and used `@agentmail/mcp-server` instead of `agentmail-mcp` — should miss this goal. Behavioral framing: accept any shell control flow (`if [ -n $AGENTMAIL_API_KEY ]`, jq pipe, jq selector, etc.) as long as the entry only appears when the env var is set.

completeness_goals.goal: Optimize layer ordering in Dockerfile.worker so frequently-changing inputs (the compiled `agent-swarm` binary COPY from the builder stage, the plugin `commands`/`agents`/`skills` COPYs, and the entrypoint COPY) appear AFTER the slow/stable layers (apt packages, Bun install, npm globals, marketplace plugin RUN, static config RUN). The COPY of the built binary should not appear before the npm globals RUN.

_provenance.verbatim: ### Phase 4: Optimize layer ordering in Dockerfile ... Reorder for maximum cache hits (least-changing first): ... 9. **Binary from builder** (changes on every code change) ... Key insight: the binary COPY and plugin file COPYs should come **after** all package installs since they change most frequently.
_provenance.note: User explicitly listed the order and called out the binary COPY + plugin COPYs as last.

completeness_goals.rationale: Detectable structurally: line position of `COPY --from=builder` relative to the `npm install -g` RUN block in the final Dockerfile.worker. Does not require the exact 11-step ordering the user listed — only the load-bearing inversion the user explained (binary/plugin COPYs after package installs). Anti-gaming: cannot pass by reordering only the npm-globals layer; the binary COPY position is the discriminator.

decomposition_notes: 1 anti-gaming guard (weight 0) + 3 core scoring goals + 4 secondary scoring goals. v3 weights: cores 0.22/0.22/0.18 (sum 0.62), secondaries 0.10/0.10/0.10/0.08 (sum 0.38), total 1.00. Cores are the three Phase 1+2+3a structural changes the user gave concrete code blocks for. Secondaries cover Phase 3b/3c bundled (wts + static dirs as one move), Turn 4 lazy tasks, Turn 4 agentmail with the AGENTMAIL_API_KEY conditional reinstated from the user's verbatim ask, and Phase 4 layer reordering. Weights deliberately do NOT mirror the test_manifest F2P vector (0.25/0.20/0.25/0.15/0.15 over a different 5-gate subset) so the rubric is not a re-skin of the test.sh gates: e.g., MCP-jq is NOT a v3 goal at all (oracle invention; the user's Turn 1 Phase 3 plan did not mention jq), and the wts/static-dir move IS a v3 goal even though it is P2P-only in test.sh. Phase 4 layer ordering (also absent from test.sh) is included because the user explicitly listed it as Phase 4 in their plan.

_provenance.user_turn_2_skip_reason: Turn 2 is a single-word 'continue' resume after an interrupt; it conveys no new task semantics and the user_simulation_prompt explicitly flags it as a minimal resume signal, not a requirement.

_self_critique_notes: Phase B self-critique then rewrite. (1) Provenance per goal — every goal_1..goal_7 carries a verbatim excerpt from instruction.md or the Turn 3/Turn 4 message in the user_simulation_prompt, and goal_0 is the rubric floor. (2) Coverage — Phase 1 (npm pin) -> goal_2; Phase 2 (apt) -> goal_1; Phase 3a (marketplace) -> goal_3; Phase 3b+3c (wts + static dirs) -> goal_4; Phase 4 (layer ordering) -> goal_7; Turn 3 (latest pins, e2e) -> reinforces goal_2; Turn 4 item 1 (agentmail) -> goal_6; Turn 4 item 2 (lazy tasks) -> goal_5. Turn 2 ('continue') intentionally not a goal. (3) No oracle-only identifiers — dropped the v1/test.sh `entrypoint_mcp_json_jq` gate entirely because re-reading instruction.md Phase 3 shows the user did NOT ask for jq (Phase 3 says 'Move static setup from entrypoint to Dockerfile'); jq was the oracle's chosen implementation. The user-pinned identifiers actually used: `wts`, `qa-use`, `pm2`, `@sentry/cli`, `@desplega.ai/localtunnel`, `npx`, `agentmail-mcp`, `AGENTMAIL_API_KEY`, `?include=tasks`. The oracle-only `fetchAgent(id, false)` is offered as one acceptable form for goal_5 alongside the behavioral fallback. (4) Pin contracts user pinned — goal_6 reinstates the AGENTMAIL_API_KEY conditional (v2 wrongly dropped it; user's verbatim Turn 4 message literally contains 'if the AGENTMAIL_API_KEY is present'). goal_3 requires both build-time presence AND entrypoint removal because user said 'Move ... Remove the entire marketplace block'. (5) Anti-gaming in goals — goal_2 forbids @latest on the user-named packages specifically (cannot bypass by pinning others), goal_3 requires both halves (cannot fake by Dockerfile-only), goal_4 requires keeping AGENT_ID-scoped dirs in entrypoint (cannot fake by wholesale deletion), goal_6 pins three contracts (npx + package name + env conditional), goal_7 uses binary-COPY position not just any reorder. (6) goal_0 anti-effort — present, weight 0, zeroes the rubric on no-op/blob-bomb/revert. (7) Weights ≠ F2P — test.sh F2P vector is {0.25, 0.20, 0.25, 0.15, 0.15} over five distinct gates that include the MCP-jq gate; v3 vector is {0.22, 0.22, 0.18, 0.10, 0.10, 0.10, 0.08} over seven gates that exclude MCP-jq and include layer-reordering + wts/static-dirs. Sum verified: 0.22+0.22+0.18+0.10+0.10+0.10+0.08 = 1.00.

_dropped_from_v2.reason: Oracle-specific identifier. Re-reading instruction.md Phase 3, the user wrote 'Move static setup from entrypoint to Dockerfile' (marketplace, wts, dirs) — jq was NOT in the user's Phase 3 plan. v1's rationale that 'Phase 3 of user Turn 1 plan: Refactor MCP JSON generation to use jq' is factually wrong w.r.t. the instruction text. v2 softened it to 'jq, factored helper, or equivalent structured construction' but still listed it as a core goal — that retains an oracle bias since the user never asked for any MCP refactor mechanism in Turn 1. The closest the user comes is Turn 4's agentmail block, which v3 captures via goal_6 (the AGENTMAIL_API_KEY conditional implicitly demands non-heredoc construction or shell control flow). Removing the standalone jq goal eliminates the largest source of v1/v2/test.sh oracle bias.

_added_for_coverage.reason: User's instruction.md Phase 4 is an explicit, named phase of the requested plan ('Optimize layer ordering in Dockerfile'). Neither v1 nor v2 had a goal for it — both stopped at Phase 3. v3 adds it as a 0.08-weight secondary goal because it is genuinely user-asked and behaviorally checkable (binary-COPY line position relative to npm-globals RUN).

_self_critique_log.gemini_rationale: Goals lack explicit 'NOT satisfied by commented-out code / stubs' language; biggest risk is an agent commenting out entrypoint blocks (goal_3/goal_4) instead of deleting them.
_self_critique_log.fix: Strengthened goal_0 prohibitions list to zero the rubric when docker-entrypoint.sh changes are only comment-outs/heredoc fences/`if false` guards rather than real deletions. Added explicit 'NOT satisfied by commented-out code or dead-code stubs' clauses to goal_3 (marketplace block) and goal_4 (wts + static dirs) naming the specific cheating patterns (`#` prefix, `: <<'EOF'` heredoc, `if false; then`, `:`/`true`/`echo` stubs). Goal weights unchanged; rubric weight sum remains 1.00.

flags.internet_with_live_origin: Dockerfile has live github.com clone AND task.toml sets allow_internet=true. Allows git-ops exfiltration of merged upstream fix.
flags.unpinned_python_deps: Dockerfile pip installs unpinned packages (e.g., pyyaml). Reproducibility risk.
